name: --warmup
-w
options.description: Perform warmupruns (number) before the actual benchmarking starts

name: --min-runs
-m
options.description: Perform at least NUM runs for each command

name: --max-runs
-M
options.description: Perform at most NUM runs for each command. Default: no limit

name: --runs
-r
options.description: Perform exactly NUM runs for each command

name: --setup
-s
options.description: Execute cmd once before each set of timing runs

name: --prepare
-p
options.description: Execute cmd before each timing run. This is useful for clearing disk caches, for example

name: --cleanup
-c
options.description: Execute cmd after the completion of all benchmarking runs for each individual command to be benchmarked

name: --parameter-scan
-P
options.description: Perform benchmark runs for each value in the range min..max

name: --parameter-step-size
-D
options.description: This argument requires --parameter-scan to be specified as well. Traverse the range min..max in steps of delta

name: --parameter-list
-L
options.description: Perform benchmark runs for each value in the comma-separated list of values

options.name: --style
options.description: Set output style type

name: --shell
-S
options.description: Set the shell to use for executing benchmarked commands

name: --ignore-failure
-i
options.description: Ignore non-zero exit codes of the benchmarked commands

name: --time-unit
-u
options.description: Set the time unit to use for the benchmark results

options.name: --export-asciidoc
options.description: Export the timing summary statistics as an AsciiDoc table to the given file

options.name: --export-csv
options.description: Export the timing summary statistics as CSV to the given file

options.name: --export-json
options.description: Export the timing summary statistics and timings of individual runs as JSON to the given file

options.name: --export-markdown
options.description: Export the timing summary statistics as a Markdown table to the given file

options.name: --show-output
options.description: Print the stdout and stderr of the benchmark instead of suppressing it

options.name: --command-name
options.description: Identify a command with the given name

options.name: --help
options.description: Prints help message

options.name: --version
options.description: Shows version information
